userStories.title: Config & Auth — Store API credentials locally
userStories.description: Build a config store using `conf` to persist API URL and API key to ~/.koda/config.json. Implement auth resolution with precedence: CLI flag → environment variable → config file. Provide commands to login, show config, and update config.
acceptanceCriteria: koda login --api-key <key> [--api-url <url>] saves to ~/.koda/config.json
koda config show displays masked API key (e.g., sk-proj-****...)
koda config set [--api-key <key>] [--api-url <url>] updates config file
koda version displays CLI version from package.json
resolveAuth() precedence: flag > env > config > default (http://localhost:3100/api)
KODA_API_KEY and KODA_API_URL environment variables override config file
Config file permissions are restrictive (mode 600 or equivalent)
API key validation before saving (non-empty, reasonable length)

userStories.title: Output Utilities — Format human & machine-readable output
userStories.description: Build output utilities in utils/output.ts for pretty-printing and utils/error.ts for exit code handling. Support --json flag for machine-readable JSON on all commands. Provide chalk-colored success/error/warn helpers and column-aligned table formatter.
acceptanceCriteria: output(data, { json?: boolean }) prints JSON if json=true, else pretty-printed format
table(headers, rows) renders column-aligned table with chalk-colored headers
success(msg), error(msg), warn(msg) print colored status messages
Exit codes: 0 (success), 1 (API error), 2 (config error), 3 (validation error)
handleApiError(err) logs API error and exits with code 1
All human-readable output goes to stdout; errors go to stderr
--json flag produces valid parseable JSON on all commands
Table headers are bold/cyan; alternating row colors optional

userStories.title: Project Commands — List and view projects
userStories.description: Implement project list and show commands using the generated API client. Support --json flag for machine output. Projects display as a table with Name, Key, and Slug columns.
acceptanceCriteria: koda project list [--json] returns all projects
koda project show <slug> [--json] returns single project details
Human output displays projects in aligned table format
--json output returns valid JSON array/object
API errors are caught and handled with proper exit codes
Unauthenticated requests fail gracefully with clear error message

userStories.title: Ticket Commands — Full lifecycle CLI ticket management
userStories.description: Implement all ticket CRUD and state-machine commands: create, list, show, verify, assign, start, fix, verify-fix, close, reject, open. Support filtering (status, type, priority, assignee), pagination, and state transitions with required comments. Display tickets in aligned table and detailed view formats.
acceptanceCriteria: koda ticket create --project <slug> --type bug|enhancement --title "..." [--desc "..."] [--priority low|medium|high|critical] [--json] creates ticket

koda ticket list --project [--status ] [--type ] [--priority

] [--assigned-to ] [--unassigned] [--limit 20] [--page 1] [--json] returns filtered list
koda ticket mine [--project <slug>] [--status <s>] [--json] returns tickets assigned to current agent
koda ticket show <ref> [--json] displays full ticket details (ref = KODA-42 or CUID)
koda ticket verify <ref> --comment "..." transitions CREATED → VERIFIED with VERIFICATION comment
koda ticket assign <ref> [--to <agent-slug>] assigns ticket (omit --to = self-assign)
koda ticket start <ref> transitions VERIFIED → IN_PROGRESS
koda ticket fix <ref> --comment "..." [--git-ref "v1.0:src/auth.ts:42"] transitions IN_PROGRESS → VERIFY_FIX with FIX_REPORT comment
koda ticket verify-fix <ref> --comment "..." [--pass|--fail] transitions VERIFY_FIX → CLOSED (--pass) or VERIFY_FIX → IN_PROGRESS (--fail) with REVIEW comment
koda ticket close <ref> manually closes ticket
koda ticket reject <ref> --comment "..." rejects ticket (from any state) with GENERAL comment
koda ticket open <ref> opens ticket in browser
Ticket list displays table with columns: #, Type, Priority, Status, Assignee, Title
Ticket show displays full details including creation/update timestamps and all comments
All state transitions enforce required comment types via API state-machine validation
Invalid transitions fail with clear error message
--json flag produces valid JSON on all commands

userStories.title: Comment & Agent Commands — Add comments and view current agent
userStories.description: Implement comment add command to post comments on tickets with optional type (verification, fix_report, review, general). Implement agent me command to display current authenticated agent.
acceptanceCriteria: koda comment add <ref> --body "..." [--type verification|fix_report|review|general] posts comment to ticket
koda agent me [--json] displays current agent's profile (name, slug, api_key masked)
Comment type defaults to general if omitted
API validates comment type against allowed values
--json output is parseable JSON

userStories.title: CLI Entry Point & Integration — Bootstrap program and register all commands
userStories.description: Create index.ts with shebang, initialize Commander program, register all command modules, and ensure proper error handling throughout. Integrate all stories into a cohesive CLI interface.
acceptanceCriteria: #!/usr/bin/env node shebang in index.ts for shell execution
program.name('koda'), .description(...), .version(...) configured
All command modules (login, config, project, ticket, comment, agent) are registered
bun run build in packages/cli passes without errors
bun run type-check passes (zero TypeScript errors)
bun run lint passes (zero warnings, max-warnings=0)
bun run test passes with 80%+ coverage
Global error handling for uncaught exceptions
Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM
package.json bin field points to ./dist/index.js
koda --help displays all commands and global options
